setting.note: Miłosz's lyrical novel of boyhood follows Thomas, growing up on his grandparents' manor in the Issa valley of Lithuania in the years after the First World War, a world of forests, folk belief, hunting and the tangled loyalties of Poles, Lithuanians and Jews. He drew the valley from his own childhood at Szetejnie on the Nevėžis: the manor and its estate, the slow river and its meadows, the deep forests full of the old pagan spirits, and the village church.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Czesław Miłosz, first published 1955 (Dolina Issy). Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Issa Valley, Czesław Miłosz (1955). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author's estate or publisher.

palette.note: The green of the Lithuanian valley and its forests, the pantheistic natural world at the heart of Thomas's boyhood. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
